rationale: The two papers are tightly aligned on the same core phenomenon (online review polarity/extremity): the cited 2020 JMR paper studies prevalence/drivers of polarity and self-selection, while the 2022 citing paper explicitly positions its contribution as adding an attrition mechanism to prior utility/base-rate explanations. Given this direct conceptual overlap and likely use as a mechanism/foundational comparison point, this is not an obvious incidental mention.
